title: TypeScript Syntax
content:
### Basic Syntax Rules

TypeScript syntax is very similar to JavaScript with the addition of type annotations.

### Case Sensitivity

TypeScript is case-sensitive. `name` and `Name` are different variables.

### Statements

Statements are separated by semicolons (optional in most cases due to automatic semicolon insertion):

```typescript
let x: number = 5;
let y: number = 10;
```

### Whitespace

TypeScript ignores whitespace, tabs, and newlines. You can format code freely.

### Identifiers

Identifiers are names given to variables, functions, classes, etc. Rules:

| Rule | Description |
|------|------------|
| First character | Must be a letter, underscore, or dollar sign |
| Subsequent characters | Can include letters, digits, underscores, dollars |
| Reserved words | Cannot use keywords like `let`, `const`, `class` |

### Blocks

Blocks group statements using curly braces:

```typescript
if (true) {
  let x: number = 10;
  console.log(x);
}
```

### Comments

```typescript
// Single-line comment

/* Multi-line
   comment */
```

> **Key Point:** TypeScript syntax extends JavaScript syntax with type annotations.
